observations.recommendation:
Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the management
direct the following officers to:
The Municipal Accountant:
1) Conduct regular reconciliation of PPE accounts with the records of the GSO;
2) Coordinate with the appropriate departments in order to trace and verify the
complete information of the listed PPE;
3) After the count, trace any discrepancy with the RPCPPE and perform proper
derecognition and disposition procedures, together with other concerned
personnel, for non-existing/missing PPE; and
4) Ensure that the accounting procedures are done in a timely manner through
proper time management and sound internal control in order to achieve a fair
presentation of financial statements.
The General Services Officer:
1) Conduct an annual physical inventory of all supplies and properties of the local
government unit as of December 31 of each year, as mandated by the pertinent
regulations; and
2) Maintain and monitor Property Cards (PC) in order to have sound internal
control over the monitoring of the PPEs.
We also recommended that the management conduct the “One Time Cleansing” of
PPE account balances of the Municipality in accordance with COA Circular 2020-
0062.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the management direct the following
officials to the subsequent courses of action:
Municipal Accountant:
1) Support the journal voucher (JV) with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for
Roads, working papers on the distribution of costs for the road components,
and working papers on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost
for road components with no available cost per registry; and
2) Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every
road network.
Municipal General Services Officer:
1) Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map;
2) Conduct a physical count and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the
Road Network System;
3) Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and their
components; and
4) Prepare the Report on the Local Road Network of the MLGU of Libungan.
Municipal Engineer:
Provide the Municipal Accountant and the GSO with the complete description and
cost segregation of road components for road projects.

In light of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following courses of
actions:
a) That the Municipal Accountant review and reclassify all semi-expendable
properties erroneously recorded under Other Supplies and Materials Inventory
to the appropriate semi-expendable property accounts, in accordance with COA
Circular No. 2024-006;
b) Perform a thorough reconciliation of all semi-expendable property acquisitions
for CY 2024 and prior years to identify and correct any remaining unclassified
items that may have been inadvertently left out during initial reclassification;
c) Ensure that Property Cards, Ledger Cards, Registry of Semi-Expendable
Property Issued (RegSPI), and Report of Semi-Expendable Property Issued
(RSPI) are maintained for all semi-expendable properties, regardless of
immediate issuance, to ensure proper documentation and monitoring; and,
d) Conduct a physical inventory of all semi-expendable properties and submit the
required Report on the Physical Count of Semi-Expendable Property (RPCSP)
in a timely manner to comply with existing accounting and auditing
requirements.

The General Services Officer:

We also recommended that the management establish full internal control over the Petty Cash Fund by formulating a policy on the disbursement and replenishment of the fund.

observations.recommendation:
Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended the management to direct the
ensuing personnel the enumerated course of actions:
To the Municipal Accountant:
a. Compute or trace any discrepancy with the RPCPPE and prepare proper adjusting
entries to correct the same;
b. Maintain a manual PPE Ledger Cards to record the custody, estimated useful life,
depreciation, impairment and disposal of those assets; and
c. Ensure that the accounting procedures were done in a timely manner, through proper
time management and sound internal control, in order to achieve a fair presentation of
financial statements.
To the Municipal General Service Officer:
a. Maintain proper and complete records of the PPEs, particularly the stock cards and
property cards as stipulated under the Government Accounting Manual for LGU Vol 1;
b. Conduct proper procedures to dispose of the unserviceable properties, in coordination
with the accounting department to derecognize the same in the books of the agency;
c. Ensure that any information included in the report presents fair and accurate data that
is reliable and verifiable against supporting documents.; and
d. Institute measures in the monitoring and controlling of the properties of the LGU.

With the foregoing observations, we recommended the following:
a) Revisit current accounting practices and institute reforms to comply with the prescribed
accounting standards under International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) and
Government Accounting Manual for LGUs;
b) Identify items that requires to be reclassified as PPE and those small tangible items that
needs for derecognition since these inventories were already distributed to the end-users;
c) Require the Office of the DRRM to submit the liquidation report for the distribution of
food supplies to the Office of the Municipal Accountant; and
d) Devise a uniform system of procedure in the monitoring, controlling and recording of
acquisition and issuance of inventory.

We recommended for the management to:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare journal entries to correct the entries previously
made, record the loss from sale, and derecognize the corresponding asset.
b) Enjoin the concerned department heads to comply with the applicable laws, rules and
regulations on the disposal of unserviceable properties.
c) Ensure that the accounting procedures were done in a timely manner and sound internal
control in order to achieve the fair presentation of financial statements.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare detailed action plan to ensure the timely
submission of the vouchers to the Office of the Auditor. Further, the Local Chief Executive
should issue the necessary directive to inform and require all departments to adhere and
commit to the action plan and proposed improvements;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to strategize to get a copy of the bank statement as of
each prescribed period by requesting from its depository banks or using the online banking
facilities provided by LBP. Moreover, ensure that bank reconciliation is done within the
prescribed period as stated in the above-cited regulations; and
c) Enjoined the LGU department heads to comply with the applicable laws, rules and
regulations on the preparation and submission of various financial reports and documents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to:
a) Instruct the Municipal Accountant and MSWDO to submit the accomplished SAC Forms,
barangay certification, printed copy of properly labeled pictures of the beneficiaries during
the actual receipt of the assistance. Failure to submit the required reports shall merit an
issuance Notice of Suspension from the Commission;
b) Ensure that all disbursement vouchers and report of disbursements be properly supported
with complete documentations before submitting the same to the audit team; and
c) Comply with the requirements set forth under the above-cited regulations for the payment
and liquidations of SAP-ESP.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the concerned department heads to comply with the accounting and reporting requirements set forth under the COA Circular 2015-008 as follows:
1. Municipal Accounting Office – to obtain necessary data from Engineering Office and reconcile the difference as reported in the FS and the report of local road network system, then prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger cards for Local Road Network, provide depreciation separately to each component of the road network with no provision for residual value and disclose the road network system in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the prescribed format.
2. Municipal General Services Office – to maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map, keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card, as well as prepare a Report on Local Road Network as part of its duties and responsibilities;
3. Inventory Committee – to promptly conduct an inventory of the road network system, and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System; and
4. Municipal Engineering Office – to provide the Accounting Office and GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to facilitate the preparation of the required documents from the offices concerned.
observations.reason: Necessary records are still not maintained by the Management. Non-disclosure in the FS of the Local Road Network System

department heads to comply with the accounting and reporting requirements set forth
under the COA Circular 2015-008 as follows:

to obtain necessary data from Engineering Office and reconcile the difference as reported in the FS and the report of local road network
system, then prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger cards for Local Road Network,
provide depreciation separately to each component of the road network with no
provision for residual value and disclose the road network system in the Notes to
Financial Statements in accordance with the prescribed format.

the Electronic Accounting System employed by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Inventory
Committee to conduct the mandatory annual physical count of the property, plant and equipment and submit
the RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor copy furnished the Municipal Accountant as a basis for
reconciliation to the books. Also, instruct the GSO and the Accountant to reconcile the records
with the physical count and appropriate adjustments should be made to account for the
unserviceable, obsolete, and lost properties.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Engineer to see
to it that all government PPAs especially infrastructure projects are publicized. Adhere strictly to
the provision set forth under COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

a. Adhere strictly with the rules and regulations for the granting, utilization and liquidation
of cash advances as prescribed under Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 97-002.
b. Send demand letters to the concerned employees. If no settlement or satisfactory
explanation is received within thirty (30) days, cause the withholding of salary due to the
accountable officer/s.
c. Refrain from granting additional cash advances to accountable officers who have
previous unsettled cash advances.
d. The Head of the Agency should file a request for authority to write-off the dormant
unliquidated cash advances and follow the specific procedures as required under COA
Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that employees will receive no
less than the threshold prescribed in the General Appropriations Act which is , P5,000.00 for the
ensuing year 2018; evaluate requests for deductions against salaries for the payment of loans and
not permit if such will lower the employees net take home pay below the threshold, instead,
advise employees to personally settle/directly remit dislodged payments for the loans from
private lending institutions over the counter.

We recommended that the Members of the Municipal Local School Board:
a. Observe strictly the requirement set forth in Section 7 (b) of the Republic Act No. 10410
by allocating in Local School Board Budget adequate funds for various activities in
support of the implementation of the ECCD Program, and comply with the guidelines on
allowable charges against the Special Education Fund set forth in Section 4 of Joint
Circular No. 1. s.2017 of DepED, DBM and DILG; and
b. Conduct consultation/discussion/hearing with the local ECCD Council, Child
Development Teachers, Day Care Workers and other stakeholders of the ECCD System
in planning and budget preparation for the implementation of the ECCD programs.

We recommended that the management require the Municipals Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report duly approved by the head of
the procuring entity and submit the same to the Government Procurement and Policy Board
(GPPB) in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each
semester. Also, make use of this report as a tool in monitoring, assessing and improving the
procurement processes.

We recommended that the Sanggunian Bayan shall pass a resolution properly authorizing the
Municipality of Libungan to open a separate bank account for the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended to the management to limit the incurrence of the expenditures to the
amounts appropriated in the budget, the availability of cash for the period and avoid utilizing the
amount appropriated for the remittance of mandatory obligations. Prepare realistic budget and
increase collections to provide sufficient cash for the conduct of regular operations of the LGU.
Monitor cash utilization and install adequate internal control to observe the handling of funds of
the LGU. Ensure that the cash overdraft will be sufficiently covered by the first collections
accruing to the General Fund on its succeeding year pursuant to government regulations.

The Management should ensure that all projects to be funded out of the 20% development fund shall partake the nature of capital expenditure and investment and record corresponding asset account/s in the financial statements

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Management to ensure that all projects to be funded out of the 20% development fund shall partake the nature of capital expenditure and investment and record corresponding asset account/s in the financial statements.

We recommend the discontinuance the use of open dump sites and identify possible sanitary landfill to develop and operate as a final disposal site for solid wastes. This landfill shall be designed and operated in accordance with the guidelines/criteria mentioned in the said act.

We recommend the LCE to direct the  Municipal Accountant to draw JEV right away for the corresponding adjustment entry of the correct amount due from taxpayers for basic and additional levy on real property taxes as well as for the fines involved.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and other officials concerned to undertake effective, efficient and aggressive tax collection strategies to improve collection efficiency.  Moreover, we recommend that Sangguniang Bayan enjoin to impose the legal remedies either by administrative auction thru levy on real property or by judicial auction to enforce the collections of the computed arrearages amounting to P26,677,387.33.

We recommend that the management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the reciprocal accounts, prepare reconciliation statement, and effect adjustments to reflect the correct amount balances, thus, present reliable data on the Municipality’s financial position as of a given period of time.

We recommend that the management should implement effective control system on the property management through the following:
The Local Chief Executive shall require the periodic physical count of property annually and the reconciliation between the accounting and property records after inventory taking.  A report thereat shall be made using the report on the physical count of property, plant and equipment (RPCPPE) which shall be submitted to the auditor concerned not later than January 31 of the ensuing year; and
The designated GSO shall prepare and issue ARE, instead MR, which shall be renewed every three (3) years; and maintain property cards which shall be regularly reconciled with the accounting records.
We recommend that the Management ensure all real estate of the LGU are registered under the Torrens Title System and responsible for their proper and effective use and management.

We recommend for the management to conduct an inventory count. And immediately thereafter prepare and submit the necessary report for the actual inventory conducted on the Work/Other Animals and Breeding Stock in order for the accounting unit to record any possible adjustments affecting the condition of the animals, to reflect a fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive to:
Identify authorized Local projects that necessitate the hiring of job order workers.
Align the hiring of job order personnel services with the Sanggunian authorized local projects.

We recommend that LGU direct the Budget Officer to be strict in considering the limitations set by law in the preparation of the budget.  It shall be mindful that appropriation serves as the authorization in the spending of funds for the personnel and in the delivery of basic services and development projects.

We recommend that LGU submit not only annual appropriation but as well as supplemental appropriation for review to the concerned authority, and observe and comply with the recommendations therein.  Also, we recommend that LGU shall always have legal basis for appropriation and obligation of allowances and/or benefits.  Items of appropriation contrary to limitations or in excess of amounts prescribed by law shall be disallowed or reduced accordingly.
We recommend that LGU submit appropriations responsive to the expenses needed in the discharge, delivery of services and development projects to the constituents.

We recommend that Management to submit the basic financial reports of the LGU within the timeframe as prescribed by the regulations for timely review and audit.  The Management shall adhere strictly at all times to the guidelines provided on the submission of accounts.

There were personnel pulled-out from the Accounting Office causing delay on the preparation of reports.
